name: Sample Warrior
godForm: Advanced form placeholder. Enable the Advanced Campaign Module if your campaign uses merged forms, ascensions, awakenings, or multi-world progression.
divinePurpose: Protect the party, hold the line, and become a legend through play.
biography: Replace this biography in the character editor. Describe where this hero came from, what they want, who they fear, and why they travel with the party.
accomplishments: Add this character's first major accomplishment here.

items.name: Heirloom Blade
items.desc: A starter item waiting for your campaign lore.

relationships.name: The Party
relationships.desc: Shared history will grow through sessions.

footerQuote: Every legend needs a first step.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.text:
Choose two Evocation spells, each of which must not be higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

Whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.text: When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on the saving throw, the target takes half the damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.text: When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell’s level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws, and take no damage if they would normally take half on a successful save.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.text: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Int. modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Overchannel
features.text:
When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1–5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it.

The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. Subsequent uses before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity. Subsequent uses before finishing a Long Rest increases the Necrotic damage per spell level by 1d12.

features.name: Core Wizard Traits
features.text:
As a Level 1 Character:

Gain all the traits in the Core Wizard Traits table.
Gain the Wizard’s level 1 features.

Core Wizard Traits



Primary Ability
Intelligence


Hit Point Die
D6 per Wizard level


Saving Throw Proficiencies
Intelligence and Wisdom


Skill Proficiencies
Choose 2: Arcana, History, Insight, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion


Weapon Proficiencies
Simple weapons


Armor Training
None


Starting Equipment
Choose A or B: (A) 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Quarterstaff), Robe, Spellbook, Scholar’s Pack, and 5 GP; or (B) 55 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.text:
As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells.
Cantrips. You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Wizard Features table.
Spellbook. Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique book: your spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or s

features.name: Ritual Adept
features.text: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn’t have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.text:
When you finish a Short Rest, you can recover expended spell slots. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than {{(classlevel/2)@roundup}}, and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher.

You can use this feature once per Long Rest.

features.name: Scholar
features.text: You can choose one of the skills in which you’re proficient and gain Expertise in it.

features.name: Wizard Subclass
features.text: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass’s features that are of your Wizard level or lower.

features.name: Memorize Spell
features.text: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells with another level 1+ spell.

features.name: 8: Ability Score Improvement
features.text: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 12 and 16.

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.text:
You’ve achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast at will. Choose a level 1 and level 2 spell in your spellbook that has a casting time of an action. You always have them prepared, and can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level from the book.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.text: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.text: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbooks. You always have these spells prepared, and can cast them each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Short or Long Rest before you can cast them in this way again.

features.name: Creature Type
features.text: You are a Humanoid.

features.name: Darkvision
features.text: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 ft.

features.name: Elven Lineage
features.text: Choose a lineage from the Elven Lineages table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.text: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.text: You have proficiency in the Insight, Perception, or Survival skill.

features.name: Trance
features.text: You don’t need to sleep, and magic can’t put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

features.name: Elven Lineage Spells
features.text:
When you choose your Elven Lineage, and at character levels 3 and 5, you learn a spell as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

features.name: Ability Score Increases
features.text: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three scores by 1.

features.name: Boon of Combat Prowess
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. One ability score of your choice increased by 1.

 Peerless Aim. Once per turn, when you miss an attack roll, you can hit instead.

features.name: Boon of Fate
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1.

 Improve Fate. When you or another creature within 60 ft. of you succeeds or fails a D20 Test, you can roll 2d4 and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the d20 roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you roll Initiative or finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Boon of Spell Recall
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma increased by 1.

 Free Casting. Whenever you cast a spell with a level 1–4 spell slot, roll 1d4. If the number you roll is the same as the slot’s level, the slot isn’t expended.

features.name: Boon of Truesight
features.text:
Ability Score Increase.One ability score of your choice increased by 1.

 Truesight. You have Truesight with a range of 60 feet.

features.name: Magic Initiate (Wizard)
features.text:
Two Cantrips. You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Wizard spell list. Int., Wis., or Cha. is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells.

Level 1 Spell. Choose a level 1 spell from the Wizard spell list. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Spell Change. Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the cho

features.name: Sage Ability Score Improvements
features.text: The Sage Background allows you to choose between Constitution, Intelligence, and Wisdom. Increase one of these scores by 2 and another one by 1, or increase all three by 1. None of these increases can raise a score above 20.

features.name: Dark Bargain
features.text: At your GM's discretion, you have taken a Dark Bargain.

features.text: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.text: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbooks. You always have these spells prepared, and can cast them each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Short or Long Rest before you can cast them in this way again.

features.name: Creature Type
features.text: You are a Humanoid.

features.name: Darkvision
features.text: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 ft.

features.name: Elven Lineage
features.text: Choose a lineage from the Elven Lineages table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.text: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.text: You have proficiency in the Insight, Perception, or Survival skill.

features.name: Trance
features.text: You don’t need to sleep, and magic can’t put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

features.name: Elven Lineage Spells
features.text:
When you choose your Elven Lineage, and at character levels 3 and 5, you learn a spell as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

features.name: Ability Score Increases
features.text: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three scores by 1.

features.name: Boon of Combat Prowess
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. One ability score of your choice increased by 1.

 Peerless Aim. Once per turn, when you miss an attack roll, you can hit instead.

features.name: Boon of Fate
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1.

 Improve Fate. When you or another creature within 60 ft. of you succeeds or fails a D20 Test, you can roll 2d4 and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the d20 roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you roll Initiative or finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Boon of Spell Recall
features.text:
Ability Score Increase. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma increased by 1.

 Free Casting. Whenever you cast a spell with a level 1–4 spell slot, roll 1d4. If the number you roll is the same as the slot’s level, the slot isn’t expended.

features.name: Boon of Truesight
features.text:
Ability Score Increase.One ability score of your choice increased by 1.

 Truesight. You have Truesight with a range of 60 feet.

features.name: Magic Initiate (Wizard)
features.text:
Two Cantrips. You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Wizard spell list. Int., Wis., or Cha. is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells.

Level 1 Spell. Choose a level 1 spell from the Wizard spell list. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Spell Change. Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the cho

features.name: Sage Ability Score Improvements
features.text: The Sage Background allows you to choose between Constitution, Intelligence, and Wisdom. Increase one of these scores by 2 and another one by 1, or increase all three by 1. None of these increases can raise a score above 20.

features.name: Dark Bargain
features.text: At your GM's discretion, you have taken a Dark Bargain.

otherActions.name: Evocation Savant
otherActions.text:
Choose two Evocation spells, each of which must not be higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

Whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free.

otherActions.name: Potent Cantrip
otherActions.text: When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on the saving throw, the target takes half the damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect.

otherActions.name: Sculpt Spells
otherActions.text: When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell’s level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws, and take no damage if they would normally take half on a successful save.

otherActions.name: Empowered Evocation
otherActions.text: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Int. modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

otherActions.name: Overchannel
otherActions.text:
When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1–5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it.

The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. Subsequent uses before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity. Subsequent uses before finishing a Long Rest increases the Necrotic damage per spell level by 1d12.

otherActions.name: Core Wizard Traits
otherActions.text:
As a Level 1 Character:

Gain all the traits in the Core Wizard Traits table.
Gain the Wizard’s level 1 features.

Core Wizard Traits



Primary Ability
Intelligence


Hit Point Die
D6 per Wizard level


Saving Throw Proficiencies
Intelligence and Wisdom


Skill Proficiencies
Choose 2: Arcana, History, Insight, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion


Weapon Proficiencies
Simple weapons


Armor Training
None


Starting Equipment
Choose A or B: (A) 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Quarterstaff), Robe, Spellbook, Scholar’s Pack, and 5 GP; or (B) 55 GP

otherActions.name: Spellcasting
otherActions.text:
As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells.
Cantrips. You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Wizard Features table.
Spellbook. Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique book: your spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or s

otherActions.name: Ritual Adept
otherActions.text: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn’t have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

otherActions.name: Arcane Recovery
otherActions.text:
When you finish a Short Rest, you can recover expended spell slots. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than {{(classlevel/2)@roundup}}, and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher.

You can use this feature once per Long Rest.

otherActions.name: Scholar
otherActions.text: You can choose one of the skills in which you’re proficient and gain Expertise in it.

otherActions.name: Wizard Subclass
otherActions.text: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass’s features that are of your Wizard level or lower.

otherActions.name: Memorize Spell
otherActions.text: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells with another level 1+ spell.

otherActions.name: 8: Ability Score Improvement
otherActions.text: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 12 and 16.

otherActions.name: Spell Mastery
otherActions.text:
You’ve achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast at will. Choose a level 1 and level 2 spell in your spellbook that has a casting time of an action. You always have them prepared, and can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level from the book.

otherActions.name: Epic Boon
otherActions.text: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.
